hypothesis: EAD erosion is pace-driven (more lanes/session = lower compliance)
method: Parse 605 unique closed lanes from SWARM-LANES.md + archive. Per-session EAD compliance (expect+actual+diff non-TBD). Pearson correlation lanes/session vs EAD%. Era comparison S370-S379 vs S380+. Individual inspection of 6 non-compliant S380+ lanes.

pace_hypothesis.counter_evidence: S375 (7 lanes, 100% EAD) vs S383 (2 lanes, 0% EAD). S381 (8 lanes, 88%). Pace is uncorrelated.

bug1_stub_closure.mechanism: close_lane.py find_latest_lane_row() only searches SWARM-LANES.md, not archive. When lanes are compacted to archive between opening and closing, the function returns None, triggering stub closure path that writes empty Etc: 'intent=closure, progress=closed'. All EAD fields lost.
bug1_stub_closure.fix: Search SWARM-LANES-ARCHIVE.md as fallback in find_latest_lane_row()

bug2_substitution_failure.mechanism: re.sub(r'actual=TBD', ...) silently fails when Etc doesn't contain 'actual=TBD' placeholder. Values provided via --actual/--diff are dropped.
bug2_substitution_failure.fix: Append actual=/diff= fields when substitution target missing

preempted_abandoned.mechanism: Lane preempted by concurrent session before actual/diff could be filled. Structural — cannot be prevented by tooling.

code_fixes.fix1_archive_fallback: find_latest_lane_row() now searches both SWARM-LANES.md and SWARM-LANES-ARCHIVE.md
code_fixes.fix2_append_on_miss: actual/diff values are appended to Etc when substitution target (TBD) doesn't exist

verdict: Pace hypothesis REJECTED (r=0.010). EAD erosion is tool-mechanism-driven: 83% (5/6) caused by two close_lane.py bugs. Both bugs fixed in this session.
